name: Combine.Num

values.name: digit
values.comment: Parse a digit.

values.name: float
values.comment: Parse a float.

values.name: int
values.comment: Parse an integer.

values.name: sign
values.comment:
Parse a numeric sign, returning `1` for positive numbers and `-1`
for negative numbers.

name: Combine.Char

values.name: anyChar
values.comment:
Parse any character.

    parse anyChar "a" ==
      (Done 'a', { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse anyChar "" ==
      (Fail ["expected any character"], { input = "", position = 0 })

values.name: char
values.comment:
Parse an exact character match.

    parse (char 'a') "a" ==
      (Done 'a', { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse (char 'a') "b" ==
      (Fail ["expected 'a'"], { input = "b", position = 0 })

values.name: crlf
values.comment: Parse a `\r\n` sequence. Returns a `\n` character.

values.name: digit
values.comment: Parses any base 10 digit.

values.name: eol
values.comment: Parses an end of line character or sequence. Returns a `\n` character.

values.name: hexDigit
values.comment: Parses any base 16 digit.

values.name: lower
values.comment: Parses any lowercase character.

values.name: newline
values.comment: Parse a `\n` character.

values.name: noneOf
values.comment:
Parse a character from the given list.

    parse (noneOf ['a', 'b']) "c" ==
      (Done 'c', { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse (noneOf ['a', 'b']) "a" ==
      (Fail ["expected none of ['a','b']"], { input = "a", position = 0 })

values.name: octDigit
values.comment: Parses any base 8 digit.

values.name: oneOf
values.comment:
Parse a character from the given list.

    parse (oneOf ['a', 'b']) "a" ==
      (Done 'a', { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse (oneOf ['a', 'b']) "c" ==
      (Fail ["expected one of ['a','b']"], { input = "c", position = 0 })

values.name: satisfy
values.comment:
Parse a character matching the predicate.

    parse (satisfy ((==) 'a')) "a" ==
      (Done 'a', { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse (satisfy ((==) 'a')) "b" ==
      (Fail ["could not satisfy predicate"], { input = "b", position = 0 })

values.name: space
values.comment: Parse a space character.

values.name: tab
values.comment: Parses a `\t` character.

values.name: upper
values.comment: Parses any uppercase character.

name: Combine.Infix

binops.comment:
Join two parsers, ignoring the result of the one on the left.

    unprefix : Parser String
    unprefix = string ">" *> while ((==) ' ') *> while ((/=) ' ')

    parse unprefix "> a" == (Done "a", { input = "", position = 3 })
binops.type: Parser x -> Parser res -> Parser res

binops.comment: Variant of `Combine.map` that ignores the Parser's result.

Join two parsers, ignoring the result of the one on the right.

    unsuffix : Parser String
    unsuffix = regex "[a-z]" <* regex "[!?]"

    parse unsuffix "a!" == (Done "a", { input = "", position = 2 })
binops.type: Parser res -> Parser x -> Parser res
Parser (res -> res_) -> Parser res -> Parser res_

binops.comment:
Variant of `Combine.mapError` that replaces the Parser's error
with a List of a single string.

    parse (string "a" <?> "gimme an 'a'") "b" ==
      (Fail ["gimme an 'a'"], { input = "b", position = 0 })

binops.type: Parser res -> Parser res -> Parser res

name: Combine

unions.name: Parser
unions.comment:
A wrapper type around `ParseFn`s used to differentiate between
eager and lazy parsers.

unions.name: Result
unions.comment:
Running a `Parser` results in one of two states:

* `Done` when the parser has successfully parsed the input (or part of
it) to a result, or
* `Fail` when the parser has failed with a list of error messages.

aliases.name: ParseFn
aliases.comment:
At their core, Parsers are functions from a `Context` to a
tuple of a `Result` and a new `Context`.

    myParseFn : ParseFn Int
    myParseFn c = (Done 1, c)

    myParser : Parser Int
    myParser = Parser myParseFn

    parse myParser "a" ==
      (Done 1, { input = "a", position = 0 })

values.name: andMap
values.comment:
Sequence two parsers.

    import Maybe
    import Result
    import String

    num : Parser Int
    num = (Maybe.withDefault 0 << Result.toMaybe << String.toInt) `map` regex "[0-9]+"

    sum : Parser Int
    sum = (+) `map` (num <* string "+") `andMap` num

    parse sum "1+2" ==
      (Done 3, { input = "", position = 3 })
values.type: Parser (res -> res_) -> Parser res -> Parser res_

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Sequence two parsers by passing in the results of the first parser
to the second.
values.type: Parser res -> (res -> Parser res_) -> Parser res_

values.name: app
values.comment: Unwrap a parser so it can be applied to a context.
values.type: Parser res -> Context -> ( Result res, Context )

values.name: between
values.comment:
Parse something between two other parsers.

The parser

    between (string "(") (string ")") (string "a")

is equivalent to the parser

    string "(" *> string "a" <* string ")"
values.type: Parser left -> Parser right -> Parser res -> Parser res

values.name: bimap
values.comment: Transform both the result and error message of a parser.
values.type: (res -> res_) -> (List String -> List String) -> Parser res -> Parser res_

values.name: brackets
values.comment: Parse something between brackets.

values.name: chainl
values.comment:
Parse one or more occurences of `p` separated by `op`, recursively
apply all functions returned by `op` to the values returned by `p`. See
the `examples/Calc.elm` file for an example.
values.type: Parser res -> Parser (res -> res -> res) -> Parser res

values.name: chainr
values.comment:
Similar to `chainl` but functions of `op` are applied in
right-associative order to the values of `p`.
values.type: Parser res -> Parser (res -> res -> res) -> Parser res

values.name: choice
values.comment:
Choose between a list of parsers.

    parse (choice [string "a", string "b"]) "a" ==
      (Done "a", { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse (choice [string "a", string "b"]) "b" ==
      (Done "b", { input = "", position = 1 })

values.name: count
values.comment: Parse `n` occurences of `p`.

values.name: end
values.comment:
Fail when the input is not empty.

    parse end "" == (Done (), { input = "", position = 0 })
    parse end "a" == (Fail ["expected end of input"], { input = "a", position = 0 })

values.name: fail
values.comment: Fail without consuming any input.

values.name: many
values.comment:
Apply a parser until it fails and return a list of the results.

    parse (many (string "a")) "aaab" ==
      (Done ["a", "a", "a"], { input = "b", position = 3 })

    parse (many (string "a")) "" ==
      (Done [], { input = "", position = 0 })

values.name: many1
values.comment:
Parse at least one result.

    parse (many1 (string "a")) "a" ==
      (Done ["a"], { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse (many1 (string "a")) "" ==
      (Fail ["expected \"a\""], { input = "", position = 0 })

values.name: map
values.comment:
Transform the result of a parser.

    parse (map String.toUpper (string "a")) "a" ==
      (Done "A", { input = "", position = 1 })

values.name: mapError
values.comment:
Transform the error of a parser.

    parse (mapError (\_ -> ["bad input"]) (string "a")) "b" ==
      (Fail ["bad input"], { input = "b", position = 0 })
values.type: (List String -> List String) -> Parser res -> Parser res

values.name: maybe
values.comment:
Wrap the return value into a `Maybe`. Returns `Nothing` on failure.

    parse (maybe (string "a")) "a" ==
      (Done (Just "a"), { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse (maybe (string "a")) "b" ==
      (Done Nothing, { input = "b", position = 0 })

values.name: optional
values.comment:
Return a default value when the given parser fails.

    letterA : Parser String
    letterA = optional "a" (string "a")

    parse letterA "a" == (Done "a", { input = "", position = 1 })
    parse letterA "b" == (Done "a", { input = "b", position = 0 })

values.name: or
values.comment:
Choose between two parsers.

    parse (string "a" `or` string "b") "a" ==
      (Done "a", { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse (string "a" `or` string "b") "b" ==
      (Done "b", { input = "", position = 1 })

    parse (string "a" `or` string "b") "c" ==
      (Fail ["expected \"a\"", "expected \"b\""], { input = "c", position = 0 })
values.type: Parser res -> Parser res -> Parser res

values.name: parens
values.comment: Parse something between parentheses.

values.name: parse
values.comment: Parse a string.
values.type: Parser res -> String -> ( Result res, Context )

values.name: rec
values.comment:
Defer a parser's evaluation.

    type E = ETerm String | EList (List E)

    whitespace : Parser String
    whitespace = regex "[ \t\r\n]*"

    term : Parser E
    term = ETerm `map` (whitespace *> regex "[a-zA-Z]+" <* whitespace)

    list : Parser E
    list = rec (\() -> EList `map` (string "(" *> many (term `or` list) <* string ")"))

    parse list "" ==
      (Fail ["expected \"(\""], { input = "", position = 0 })

    parse list "()" ==
      (Done (EList []), { input = "", position = 2 })

    parse list "(a (b c))" ==
      (Done (EList [ETerm "a", EList [ETerm "b", ETerm "c"]]), { input = "", position = 9 })

values.name: regex
values.comment:
Parse a Regex match.

Regular expressions must match from the beginning of the input and their
subgroups are ignored. A `^` is added implicitly to the beginning of
every pattern unless one already exists.

    parse (regex "a+") "aaaaab" ==
      (Done "aaaaa", { input = "b", position = 5 })

values.name: sepBy
values.comment:
Parser zero or more occurences of one parser separated by another.

    parse (sepBy (string ",") (string "a")) "b" ==
      (Done [], { input = "b", position = 0 })

    parse (sepBy (string ",") (string "a")) "a,a,a" ==
      (Done ["a", "a", "a"], { input = "", position = 5 })

    parse (sepBy (string ",") (string "a")) "a,a,b" ==
      (Done ["a", "a"], { input = ",b", position = 3 })
values.type: Parser x -> Parser res -> Parser (List res)

values.name: sepBy1
values.comment: Parse one or more occurences of one parser separated by another.
values.type: Parser x -> Parser res -> Parser (List res)

values.name: skip
values.comment: Apply a parser and skip its result.

values.name: skipMany
values.comment: Apply a parser and skip its result many times.

values.name: skipMany1
values.comment: Apply a parser and skip its result at least one.

values.name: squareBrackets
values.comment: Parse something between square brackets.

values.name: string
values.comment:
Parse an exact string match.

    parse (string "hello") "hello world" ==
      (Done "hello", { input = " world", position = 5 })

    parse (string "hello") "goodbye" ==
      (Fail ["expected \"hello\""], { input = "goodbye", position = 0 })

values.name: succeed
values.comment:
Return a value without consuming any input.

    parse (succeed 1) "a" ==
      (Done 1, { input = "a", position = 0 })

values.name: while
values.comment:
Consume input while the predicate matches.

    parse (while ((/=) ' ')) "test 123" ==
      (Done "test", { input = " 123", position = 4 })
